title: malvastrum glomeratum
intro: Malvastrum glomeratum, also known as rock rose or sticky rose, is a succulent shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its thick, fleshy leaves and bright pink or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Malvastrum glomeratum include its ability to soothe sunburns, reduce inflammation, and promote skin healing, as well as its potential to support the immune system and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of Malvastrum glomeratum can be made into creams, ointments, and salves for topical use, as well as teas and infusions for internal consumption.
